products.name: TP-Link Deco XE75 (3-Pack)
products.verdict: The best overall mesh system — Wi-Fi 6E tri-band coverage for up to 7,200 sq ft at a fair price.
products.description: The TP-Link Deco XE75 brings Wi-Fi 6E to the masses at a price that doesn't sting. The 3-pack blankets up to 7,200 sq ft with tri-band coverage, using the dedicated 6GHz band as a wireless backhaul to keep your 2.4GHz and 5GHz bands free for devices. AI-Driven Mesh intelligently routes traffic, and the Deco app makes setup a breeze. With 200+ device support and built-in HomeShield security, it's the complete package.
pros: Wi-Fi 6E at a very competitive price
Dedicated 6GHz backhaul keeps other bands fast
Covers large homes with no dead zones
Deco app setup takes under 10 minutes
cons: No USB port for NAS sharing

products.name: Amazon eero Pro 6E (3-Pack)
products.verdict: The easiest mesh system to set up — tri-band Wi-Fi 6E with TrueMesh and built-in Zigbee smart home hub.
products.description: The eero Pro 6E is the mesh system for people who hate networking. Setup takes literally 5 minutes via the eero app, and TrueMesh technology dynamically routes traffic through the fastest path. Tri-band Wi-Fi 6E covers up to 6,000 sq ft, and the built-in Zigbee smart home hub connects compatible devices without a separate bridge. Thread and Matter support future-proof your smart home ecosystem.
features: Built-in Zigbee + Thread smart home hub
pros: Easiest setup of any mesh system — 5 minutes
Built-in Zigbee hub eliminates separate bridges
Thread + Matter support is future-proof
TrueMesh routing is reliable and fast
cons: eero Secure subscription required after trial
2x Ethernet ports per node limits wired devices

products.name: Google Nest Wifi Pro (3-Pack)
products.verdict: The best mesh for Google homes — Wi-Fi 6E with Google Assistant, Matter support, and sleek design.
products.description: The Google Nest Wifi Pro integrates seamlessly into Google's smart home ecosystem. Each node covers up to 2,200 sq ft with Wi-Fi 6E tri-band performance, and the 3-pack blankets up to 6,600 sq ft. Matter and Thread support make it a smart home hub, and Google Home app management is intuitive. The minimalist design blends into any room, and automatic software updates keep security current without any effort.
pros: Gorgeous design that blends into any decor
Deep Google Home integration
Matter + Thread for smart home future-proofing
Automatic updates mean zero maintenance
cons: No Ethernet backhaul option on satellite nodes
Google Home app required — no web interface

products.name: Netgear Orbi RBKE963 Wi-Fi 6E
products.verdict: The ultimate no-compromise mesh system — 10 Gbps backhaul, quad-band, and coverage for mansions.
products.description: The Netgear Orbi RBKE963 is the most powerful mesh system money can buy. Its quad-band architecture dedicates an entire 6GHz band to wireless backhaul, leaving three bands free for devices. A 10 Gbps Ethernet port on the router handles multi-gig internet plans, and the 3-pack covers up to 9,000 sq ft. If you have a large home, gigabit+ internet, and demand the absolute fastest Wi-Fi everywhere, this is it — but you'll pay dearly for it.
pros: Quad-band with dedicated backhaul is the fastest architecture
10 Gbps port for multi-gig internet
9,000 sq ft coverage handles any home
Wired backhaul option for maximum reliability
cons: Nodes are large and not discreet
Netgear Armor subscription adds ongoing cost

products.name: TP-Link Deco X55 (3-Pack)
products.verdict: The best value mesh system — reliable Wi-Fi 6 whole-home coverage for under $200.
products.description: The TP-Link Deco X55 delivers rock-solid mesh Wi-Fi 6 at a price that's hard to argue with. The 3-pack covers up to 6,500 sq ft with dual-band AX3000 speeds, and each node has 3 Gigabit Ethernet ports for wired devices. AI-Driven Mesh learns your network habits and optimizes routing, while HomeShield provides basic network security. For homes that don't need Wi-Fi 6E, this is the sweet spot of price and performance.
pros: Unbeatable value under $200 for 3-pack mesh
6,500 sq ft coverage eliminates dead zones
3 Ethernet ports per node is generous
Deco app is beginner-friendly

products.name: ASUS ZenWiFi XT9 (2-Pack)
products.verdict: The power user's mesh system — AiMesh, AiProtection Pro, and deep customization for networking enthusiasts.
products.description: The ASUS ZenWiFi XT9 is built for users who want both ease of use and deep control. Tri-band AX7800 speeds with a dedicated 5GHz backhaul keep traffic flowing, while ASUS AiProtection Pro (powered by Trend Micro) provides lifetime free security without subscriptions. AiMesh compatibility means you can mix and match with other ASUS routers, and the ASUS Router app offers granular controls that rival full router firmware.
features: AiMesh compatible with other ASUS routers
pros: AiProtection Pro security with no subscription
Tri-band with dedicated backhaul
2.5G WAN port for faster internet plans
Deep customization via ASUS Router app
cons: Only 2 nodes — may need a 3rd for very large homes

products.name: Amazon eero 6+ (3-Pack)
products.verdict: The best budget mesh with smart home features — Wi-Fi 6, Zigbee hub, and dead-simple setup under $200.
products.description: The eero 6+ brings the simplicity of eero to budget-conscious buyers. The 3-pack covers up to 4,500 sq ft with dual-band Wi-Fi 6, and each node includes a built-in Zigbee smart home hub for connecting compatible devices. Setup takes minutes via the eero app, and TrueMesh routing keeps connections stable as you move around the house. It's the easiest way to get whole-home Wi-Fi with smart home integration on a budget.
pros: Under $200 for 3-pack mesh with Zigbee
eero app is the simplest in the industry
Built-in Zigbee hub eliminates separate bridges
Thread support for future smart home devices
cons: 4,500 sq ft is smaller than TP-Link competitors
eero Secure subscription for advanced features

products.name: TP-Link Deco BE65 (3-Pack)
products.verdict: The first affordable Wi-Fi 7 mesh system — future-proof speeds with MLO and 320 MHz channels.
products.description: The TP-Link Deco BE65 is one of the first Wi-Fi 7 mesh systems at a price that isn't stratospheric. BE11000 tri-band speeds, Multi-Link Operation (MLO) for simultaneous multi-band connections, and 320 MHz channel support deliver genuinely next-gen performance. The 3-pack covers up to 6,500 sq ft, and a 2.5G Ethernet port on each node handles faster internet plans. If you're future-proofing your home network, this is the entry point.
pros: Wi-Fi 7 at a reasonable price point
MLO provides more reliable connections
2.5G Ethernet on every node
Future-proof for years of device upgrades
cons: Benefits won't be fully realized until device ecosystem catches up

products.name: Linksys Atlas Max 6E (MX8503)
products.verdict: Enterprise-grade mesh for large homes — Wi-Fi 6E with Qualcomm chipset and Cognitive Mesh technology.
products.description: The Linksys Atlas Max 6E brings enterprise Qualcomm networking silicon to the home. Each node packs a quad-core Qualcomm processor and tri-band Wi-Fi 6E (AXE8400), with Cognitive Mesh technology that uses machine learning to optimize channel selection, band steering, and client routing. The 3-pack covers up to 9,000 sq ft, and a 5G Ethernet port handles ultra-fast internet plans. It's the choice for tech-savvy users who want maximum throughput.
pros: Qualcomm chipset delivers top-tier performance
9,000 sq ft coverage handles very large homes
5G Ethernet for multi-gig internet
Cognitive Mesh learns and optimizes over time
cons: Linksys app is less polished than eero or Deco

products.name: ASUS ZenWiFi AX (XT8) 2-Pack
products.verdict: The most reliable Wi-Fi 6 mesh — battle-tested with free lifetime security and strong Ethernet backhaul.
products.description: The ASUS ZenWiFi AX XT8 has been the go-to reliable mesh system since its launch and continues to deliver outstanding performance. Tri-band AX6600 with a dedicated 5GHz backhaul ensures satellites maintain full speed, and AiProtection Pro provides free lifetime network security without any subscription. With coverage up to 5,500 sq ft and a 2.5G WAN port, it remains one of the best values in tri-band mesh networking.
pros: Battle-tested reliability over years of updates
Free lifetime security — no subscription
Dedicated backhaul keeps speeds consistent
2.5G WAN for faster internet plans
cons: 2-pack may not cover very large homes
Firmware updates can occasionally cause issues
